title: Session Envelope Law And Fail-Closed Validation
rationale: Phase 2 must not generate tutoring behavior unless the runtime can prove guardian-started, one-child, lesson-scoped session state. This bead hardens the substrate entry point so all later tutoring acts inherit fail-closed validation.
focus_hint: Start with schema and validation boundaries. Reject missing child identity, missing guardian authorization, missing lesson binding, and any attempt to open multi-child context in child mode.
acceptance_criteria: Runtime defines a canonical session envelope type with child_id, guardian actor, lesson binding, and status
Tutor execution path fails closed when the envelope is absent, stale, cross-child, or lesson-free
Child mode retrieval remains scoped to the active child only
Projection layer exposes enough envelope state for downstream playback and tutoring without widening scope
